name: Barbell Back Squat
formCues: Set the bar across your upper back (not your neck) and grip just outside your shoulders.
Stand with feet shoulder-width, toes turned out slightly.
Brace your core like you're about to be poked in the stomach, then sit down and back.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel, keeping your whole foot planted.
Drive up through mid-foot, knees tracking over your toes.
commonMistakes: Knees caving inward on the way up — push them out over your toes.
Heels lifting off the floor; if mobility limits depth, elevate your heels slightly.
Chest collapsing forward — keep your brace and let your hips and chest rise together.

name: Goblet Squat
formCues: Hold one dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands under the top head.
Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out.
Sit straight down between your knees, elbows tracking inside them.
Keep your chest tall against the counterweight, then stand by pushing the floor away.
commonMistakes: Letting the dumbbell drift away from the chest — it should stay in contact.
Cutting depth short; the goblet position usually allows a deep, comfortable squat.
Rocking onto the toes instead of keeping the whole foot planted.

name: Bodyweight Squat
formCues: Stand with feet shoulder-width, arms reaching forward for balance.
Sit down and back as if onto a chair, keeping your heels down.
Go as deep as you can with a tall chest.
Stand up by driving through your whole foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Leaning far forward and turning it into a hip hinge.
Rushing the reps instead of controlling the descent.

name: Leg Press
formCues: Place feet shoulder-width on the platform, mid-height.
Lower the platform under control until your knees approach your chest without your lower back rounding off the pad.
Press through your whole foot without locking your knees out hard.
commonMistakes: Going so deep the hips and lower back curl off the pad.
Slamming into knee lockout at the top.
Letting the knees cave inward under load.

name: Conventional Deadlift
formCues: Stand with the bar over mid-foot, feet hip-width.
Hinge down and grip just outside your legs; shins touch the bar.
Flatten your back, pull the slack out of the bar, and brace.
Push the floor away and stand up, keeping the bar against your legs.
Lock out tall — hips through, shoulders back — then lower with control.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back — reset your brace before every pull.
Letting the bar drift away from the body, which turns it into a back lift.
Jerking the bar off the floor instead of pulling the slack out first.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
formCues: Start standing with the bar at your hips, knees softly bent.
Push your hips straight back and slide the bar down your thighs.
Lower until you feel a deep hamstring stretch, back flat the whole way.
Drive your hips forward to stand, squeezing your glutes at the top.
commonMistakes: Bending the knees more as the set gets hard, turning it into a squat.
Rounding the upper back to chase extra depth.
Pulling with the arms instead of moving from the hips.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
formCues: Hold dumbbells in front of your thighs, knees softly bent.
Push your hips back and let the dumbbells track down the front of your legs.
Stop at a strong hamstring stretch with a flat back.
Stand by driving the hips forward, not by lifting the chest first.
commonMistakes: Squatting the weight down instead of hinging.
Letting the dumbbells drift forward away from the legs.
Hyperextending the lower back at the top.

name: Glute Bridge
formCues: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat and close to your hips.
Push through your heels and lift your hips until your body is a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top for a second.
Lower with control.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back instead of finishing with the glutes.
Pushing through the toes.
Bouncing reps without a pause at the top.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
formCues: Sit with your upper back on a bench, bar over your hips (use a pad).
Feet flat, shins vertical at the top of the movement.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your hips up to full extension.
Chin tucked, ribs down — finish with glutes, not lower back.
commonMistakes: Overarching the lower back at the top.
Placing feet too far forward so the hamstrings take over.
Cutting the range short and never reaching full hip extension.

name: Kettlebell Swing
formCues: Hinge at the hips and hike the bell back between your legs like a football snap.
Snap your hips forward; the bell floats to chest height on its own.
Arms stay relaxed — the power is all hips.
Let the bell swing back into the next hinge, keeping a flat back.
commonMistakes: Squatting and lifting with the arms instead of hinging and snapping.
Leaning back at the top of the swing.
Letting the bell pull the back round between reps.

name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge
formCues: Hold dumbbells at your sides, stand tall.
Step forward far enough that your front shin stays roughly vertical.
Lower until your back knee hovers just above the floor.
Push through the front foot to step into the next lunge.
commonMistakes: Steps too short, driving the front knee far past the toes.
Torso pitching forward over the front leg.
Pushing off the back foot instead of driving with the front leg.

name: Split Squat
formCues: Take a long stance, one foot forward and one back, hips square.
Lower straight down until the back knee nearly touches the floor.
Keep most of your weight through the front foot.
Drive back up without bouncing.
commonMistakes: Stance too narrow, turning it into a balance exercise.
Leaning forward to escape the front-leg work.
Letting the front heel rise.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
formCues: Stand a long step in front of a bench; place your rear foot on it, laces down.
Hold dumbbells at your sides and keep your torso tall with a slight forward lean.
Lower until your front thigh is near parallel.
Drive up through the whole front foot.
commonMistakes: Standing too close to the bench, cramping the front knee.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Letting the front knee dive inward.

name: Barbell Bench Press
formCues: Lie with eyes under the bar, feet planted, shoulder blades pinched back and down.
Grip a bit wider than shoulder-width; unrack and hold over your shoulders.
Lower the bar with control to your lower chest, elbows about 45° from your sides.
Press back up and slightly toward your face, keeping your shoulder blades pinned.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows straight out at 90°, which strains the shoulders.
Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Losing the shoulder-blade pinch so the shoulders roll forward at lockout.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
formCues: Lie back with a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, shoulder blades pinched.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly together without clanking them.
Lower with control until you feel a comfortable chest stretch.
Keep your feet planted and avoid arching excessively.
commonMistakes: Letting the dumbbells drift too wide on the way down.
Pressing unevenly — one arm racing ahead.
Dropping the dumbbells at the end instead of controlling them down.

name: Push-Up
formCues: Hands a bit wider than shoulders, body in one straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest to just above the floor, elbows about 45° from your sides.
Push the floor away until your arms are straight.
Keep your hips in line — no sagging, no piking.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging or piking up.
Half reps that never bring the chest near the floor.
Hands too far forward, stressing the shoulders.

name: Machine Chest Press
formCues: Set the seat so the handles line up with mid-chest.
Pinch your shoulder blades back into the pad.
Press to full extension without letting your shoulders roll forward.
Return under control until you feel a slight chest stretch.
commonMistakes: Seat set too high or low, pressing from the shoulders.
Letting the weight stack slam between reps.
Shrugging the shoulders during the press.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
formCues: Grip just outside your shoulders, bar resting on your front delts.
Brace your glutes and core so your ribs stay down.
Press the bar straight up, moving your head back slightly to clear it.
Finish with the bar over the back of your head, arms locked.
commonMistakes: Leaning back and turning it into an incline press.
Pressing the bar forward around the face instead of straight up.
Cutting the lockout short.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
formCues: Sit tall with back support, dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward.
Press up until your arms are straight overhead.
Lower with control back to ear level.
Keep your ribs down — don't arch off the pad.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back to grind out reps.
Stopping the press short of lockout.
Letting the dumbbells drift forward.

name: Pike Push-Up
formCues: From a push-up position, walk your feet in and lift your hips high into an inverted V.
Bend your elbows and lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Press back up until your arms are straight.
Keep your hips high the whole time — the more vertical, the harder the shoulders work.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips drop so it becomes a regular push-up.
Flaring the elbows straight out.
Banging the head on the floor instead of controlling the descent.

name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
formCues: Hinge to roughly 45°, back flat, bar hanging under your shoulders.
Pull the bar to your lower ribs, leading with your elbows.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower under control without letting your torso rise.
commonMistakes: Standing up and heaving with momentum on every rep.
Rounding the lower back.
Pulling with the arms only and never retracting the shoulder blades.

name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
formCues: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a bench, back flat like a table.
Let the dumbbell hang straight down, then row it to your hip.
Lead with the elbow and keep your shoulders square to the floor.
Lower slowly to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Twisting the torso to hoist the weight.
Rowing to the shoulder instead of the hip.
Shrugging instead of pulling the shoulder blade back.

name: Inverted Row
formCues: Set a bar at waist height and hang under it, body straight, heels on the floor.
Pull your chest to the bar, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower under control to straight arms.
Keep your hips in line with your shoulders throughout.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging toward the floor.
Pulling only the head and chin to the bar.
Using a kick of momentum from the legs.

name: Seated Cable Row
formCues: Sit tall with knees softly bent, grab the handle with both hands.
Pull the handle to your stomach, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together, chest proud.
Let your arms straighten fully between reps without slumping forward.
commonMistakes: Leaning far back to move the weight.
Shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Cutting the stretch short between reps.

name: Pull-Up
formCues: Hang from the bar with hands just outside shoulder-width, palms away.
Start each rep from a dead hang with your shoulders engaged.
Pull your chin over the bar, driving your elbows down to your sides.
Lower all the way down with control.
commonMistakes: Kipping or swinging for momentum.
Half reps that never reach a dead hang.
Craning the chin over the bar without actually pulling the chest up.

name: Lat Pulldown
formCues: Grip the bar just outside shoulder-width and sit with thighs secured.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar to your upper chest.
Drive the elbows down and back, squeezing the lats.
Control the bar all the way back up to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Leaning way back and rowing the weight down.
Letting the weight yank the shoulders up between reps.

name: Band Pulldown
formCues: Anchor a band overhead and kneel or sit facing it.
Pull the band down toward your collarbones, elbows driving to your sides.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Resist the band on the way back up.
commonMistakes: Standing too close so there's no tension at the top.
Pulling with the arms only.
Letting the band snap back instead of resisting it.

name: Barbell Curl
formCues: Stand tall, grip the bar at shoulder-width, arms hanging straight.
Curl the bar up while keeping your elbows pinned to your sides.
Squeeze at the top, then lower all the way to straight arms.
Keep your torso still — no rocking.
commonMistakes: Swinging the hips to heave the bar up.
Elbows drifting forward, turning it into a front raise.
Stopping high and never letting the arms straighten.

name: Dumbbell Curl
formCues: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, palms forward (or rotate as you lift).
Curl with your elbows pinned to your ribs.
Squeeze at the top, lower slowly to full extension.
commonMistakes: Swinging the weights with body momentum.
Cutting the lowering phase short.
Shrugging the shoulders into the curl.

name: Band Curl
formCues: Stand on the middle of a band, a handle or loop in each hand.
Curl up with elbows pinned to your sides.
Fight the band on the way down for a slow negative.
commonMistakes: Letting the band snap down between reps.
Leaning back to finish reps.
Elbows drifting forward.

name: Cable Triceps Pushdown
formCues: Face the cable with elbows tucked at your sides, forearms up.
Push the handle down until your arms are fully straight.
Squeeze the triceps at lockout.
Let your forearms rise back up without your elbows leaving your sides.
commonMistakes: Leaning over the cable and pressing with bodyweight.
Elbows flaring out from the sides.
Half reps that never reach lockout.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Triceps Extension
formCues: Hold one dumbbell with both hands overhead, arms straight.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows.
Stretch the triceps at the bottom, then extend back to straight arms.
Keep your elbows pointing forward, ribs down.
commonMistakes: Flaring the elbows wide.
Arching the lower back as the weight goes back.
Using the shoulders to swing the weight up.

name: Diamond Push-Up
formCues: Set up like a push-up but with hands close, thumbs and index fingers forming a diamond.
Keep elbows tracking back along your ribs as you lower.
Press back up to full lockout, body in one line.
commonMistakes: Elbows flaring wide, shifting the work off the triceps.
Hips sagging.
Shortening the range as fatigue builds.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
formCues: Stand tall with light dumbbells at your sides, elbows softly bent.
Raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, leading with the elbows.
Pause briefly, then lower slowly.
Pour-the-jug wrist position; pinkies no higher than thumbs needed.
commonMistakes: Swinging the weights up with a hip bounce.
Shrugging the traps instead of lifting with the delts.
Going far above shoulder height with heavy weight.

name: Band Lateral Raise
formCues: Stand on the band, handles at your sides.
Raise your arms out to shoulder height with soft elbows.
Lower slowly against the band's pull.
commonMistakes: Shrugging into the raise.
Letting the band yank the arms down.
Bending the elbows more as fatigue sets in.

name: Leg Extension
formCues: Set the pad on your shins just above the ankles, knees lined up with the machine's pivot.
Extend until your knees are straight.
Pause at the top, then lower with control.
commonMistakes: Kicking the weight up with momentum.
Letting the stack slam down between reps.
Lifting the hips off the seat.

name: Leg Curl
formCues: Set the pad just above your heels, knees lined up with the pivot.
Curl your heels toward your glutes.
Squeeze at the end, then return slowly to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Arching the hips to cheat the weight up.
Short, bouncy reps.
Letting the weight pull the legs straight without control.

name: Standing Calf Raise
formCues: Stand with the balls of your feet on an edge, heels hanging off.
Lower your heels to a deep stretch.
Press up as high as you can onto your toes.
Pause at the top — no bouncing.
commonMistakes: Fast bouncing out of the stretch.
Tiny range of motion.
Bending the knees to push with the quads.

name: Plank
formCues: Forearms on the floor, elbows under shoulders, body in one straight line.
Squeeze your glutes and brace your abs like a light punch is coming.
Breathe steadily — don't hold your breath.
Stop the set when your hips start to sag.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging or piking up.
Holding the breath.
Looking up and craning the neck.

name: Dead Bug
formCues: Lie on your back, arms up, knees bent over hips.
Press your lower back gently into the floor.
Lower one arm and the opposite leg toward the floor without the back arching.
Return and switch sides, slow and controlled.
commonMistakes: Lower back arching off the floor as the leg lowers.
Rushing the tempo.
Holding the breath instead of exhaling on each rep.

name: Hanging Knee Raise
formCues: Hang from the bar with shoulders engaged.
Curl your knees up toward your chest, tilting the pelvis up at the top.
Lower slowly to a dead hang without swinging.
commonMistakes: Swinging and using momentum.
Lifting only the legs without curling the pelvis.
Dropping fast and turning the set into a swing.

name: Cable Face Pull
formCues: Set the rope at upper-chest height and grab it with thumbs toward you.
Pull the rope toward your face, elbows high and wide.
Finish with hands beside your ears, squeezing the rear delts.
Return slowly, resisting the cable.
commonMistakes: Pulling to the chest like a row.
Using so much weight the torso rocks.
Letting the shoulders roll forward at the stretch.

name: Band Pull-Apart
formCues: Hold a band at shoulder height, hands shoulder-width, arms straight.
Pull the band apart until it touches your chest.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together, then return slowly.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a row.
Shrugging the shoulders up.
Letting the band snap back.

name: Dumbbell Fly
formCues: Lie back with dumbbells over your chest, palms facing each other.
Lower the weights out wide with a fixed soft bend in the elbows.
Stop at a comfortable chest stretch.
Sweep the weights back together as if hugging a barrel.
commonMistakes: Bending and pressing instead of keeping the arc.
Going too deep and straining the shoulders.
Clanking the dumbbells and losing tension at the top.

name: Dumbbell Step-Up
formCues: Stand facing a knee-height bench or box, dumbbells at your sides.
Place one whole foot on the bench and drive through it to stand up.
Control the descent back down with the same leg.
Finish all reps on one side before switching.
commonMistakes: Pushing off the floor leg instead of working the top leg.
Box too high, forcing a forward lurch.
Dropping down instead of lowering with control.

name: Hack Squat
formCues: Set your back flat against the pad and feet shoulder-width on the platform, toes slightly out.
Unlock the safeties and lower under control until your knees reach roughly 90°.
Press through your whole foot to return, keeping your lower back against the pad.
Re-engage the safeties before stepping off.
commonMistakes: Heels rising off the platform — lower the weight and work on ankle flexibility.
Letting the knees cave inward at the bottom.
Cutting depth and only doing a quarter squat.

name: Barbell Front Squat
formCues: Rest the bar across your front delts with elbows high and parallel to the floor.
Feet shoulder-width, toes turned out slightly; keep your elbows up the entire set.
Sit straight down, staying as upright as possible; the bar should feel like it wants to go forward — resist that.
Drive up through mid-foot, maintaining elbow height until you're fully standing.
commonMistakes: Elbows dropping, which dumps the bar and rounds the upper back.
Leaning forward excessively rather than staying tall.
Wrist pain from poor flexibility — try a cross-arm grip while building front-rack mobility.

name: Banded Squat
formCues: Loop a band under your feet and hold the other end at shoulder height, or loop it around your shoulders.
Stand feet shoulder-width, toes out slightly.
Squat down while the band provides added resistance on the way up.
Drive up strong through mid-foot, pushing the band overhead if overhead style.
commonMistakes: Band pulling you forward — keep your chest tall and core braced.
Narrow stance that turns it into a quad-dominant squat without glute involvement.
Letting the knees collapse inward as fatigue builds.

name: Sumo Deadlift
formCues: Take a wide stance with toes pointed out 45°, bar over mid-foot.
Push your knees out over your toes and hinge down to grip the bar with hands inside your legs.
Brace your core, wedge your hips down, and press the floor apart as you stand.
Hips and shoulders should rise at the same rate; finish tall with hips through.
commonMistakes: Hips shooting up first, turning it into a stiff-leg pull.
Knees caving inward — actively push them out through the whole lift.
Stance too narrow to get the benefit of the sumo position.

name: Good Morning
formCues: Set a light bar across your upper back as you would for a squat.
Soft bend in the knees, brace your core, then hinge at the hips pushing them back.
Lower your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, maintaining a flat back.
Drive your hips forward to stand, squeezing the glutes at the top.
commonMistakes: Loading too heavy before the pattern is established — start very light.
Rounding the lower back as you descend.
Bending the knees too much and turning it into a squat.

name: Kettlebell Deadlift
formCues: Place the kettlebell between your feet, hinge down and grip the handle with both hands.
Flat back, braced core, shins nearly vertical.
Push the floor away to stand, keeping the bell close to your legs.
Hinge back down with control, setting it down before resetting.
commonMistakes: Rounding the back to pick the bell up.
Squatting it up rather than hinging.
Letting the bell drift forward away from your center of mass.

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
formCues: Lie on your back with both knees bent, then lift one foot off the floor.
Drive through the grounded heel and raise your hips until your body is a straight line.
Squeeze your glute hard at the top for a full second.
Lower with control and repeat, keeping the raised leg still.
commonMistakes: Allowing the hip of the raised leg to drop, creating a twist.
Arching the lower back to reach full height instead of driving through the glute.
Bouncing out of the bottom without a controlled descent.

name: Barbell Reverse Lunge
formCues: Set the bar across your upper back, stand tall with feet together.
Step one foot back and lower your back knee toward the floor.
Keep your front shin roughly vertical and your torso upright.
Drive through the front foot to return to standing, then repeat on the other side.
commonMistakes: Front knee drifting far past the toes due to too short a step.
Torso leaning excessively forward.
Using the back foot to push off rather than working the front leg.

name: Incline Barbell Press
formCues: Set the bench to 30–45°, lie back with eyes under the bar and shoulder blades pinched.
Grip just outside shoulder-width and unrack over your upper chest.
Lower the bar to your upper chest with elbows at about 45° from your torso.
Press back up, keeping the shoulder blades pinned throughout.
commonMistakes: Bench set too steep, shifting the work entirely to the shoulders.
Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Elbows flaring straight out, stressing the shoulder joint.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
formCues: Set the bench to 30–45° and sit with dumbbells on your thighs.
Kick each dumbbell up as you lie back, starting with them at upper-chest level.
Press straight up without letting your lower back arch excessively off the pad.
Lower with control until you feel a gentle stretch across the upper chest.
commonMistakes: Angle too steep — above 45° it becomes more of a shoulder press.
Pressing unevenly, one arm getting ahead of the other.
Dropping the dumbbells to the floor unsafely at the end of the set.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
formCues: Grip the bar with hands about shoulder-width (not excessively narrow).
Unrack and hold the bar over your lower chest, elbows tucked close to your ribs.
Lower to your lower chest while keeping your elbows within your torso width.
Press back up through your triceps to lockout.
commonMistakes: Grip too narrow, which strains the wrists and shifts load unevenly.
Elbows flaring out — that just becomes a regular bench press.
Bar path drifting toward the face instead of tracking over the lower chest.

name: Bench Dip
formCues: Sit on the edge of a bench, hands gripping the edge beside your hips.
Slide your hips off the bench and lower them toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Go down until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Press through your hands to extend your arms and return.
commonMistakes: Feet too far out, turning it into a shoulder-dominant movement and risking impingement.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears during the press.
Half reps that barely bend the elbows.

name: Incline Push-Up
formCues: Place your hands on a bench or elevated surface, body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest to the edge of the bench with elbows at about 45°.
Press back up to straight arms.
The higher the surface, the easier the exercise — adjust height to your current strength.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging or piking up.
Hands too wide, reducing chest involvement.
Head drooping forward so the nose hits first instead of the chest.

name: Decline Push-Up
formCues: Place your feet on a bench and hands on the floor, body in a straight line.
Lower your chest toward the floor, elbows at about 45° from your sides.
Press back up to full arm extension.
Keep your core braced so hips don't sag throughout.
commonMistakes: Feet too high, which shifts the load entirely to the shoulders.
Hips piking up as you push.
Neck cranking downward — keep a neutral spine head to heels.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
formCues: Adjust the seat so the handles are at shoulder height.
Grip the handles and press overhead to full extension without locking out aggressively.
Lower under control until the handles return to shoulder height.
Keep your back in contact with the pad throughout and avoid arching.
commonMistakes: Seat too low, turning it into an incline-press angle.
Craning the neck forward as the weight gets heavy.
Shrugging the traps instead of pressing with the delts.

name: Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press
formCues: Stand with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward, feet hip-width.
Brace your core to prevent lower-back arch, then press the dumbbells straight overhead.
Bring them together slightly at the top without shrugging.
Lower with control to shoulder height before the next rep.
commonMistakes: Leaning back to push the weights forward and up rather than straight up.
Letting the core unbraced so the lumbar arches excessively.
Pressing one dumbbell ahead of the other unevenly.

name: Band Overhead Press
formCues: Stand on the center of a band with feet hip-width, holding a handle in each hand at shoulder height.
Brace your core, then press both handles straight overhead to full extension.
Lower the handles back to shoulder height under control.
Keep the band tension even on both sides throughout.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively as the band gets harder to press at the top.
Uneven foot placement giving one side more tension than the other.
Band snap-back — resist the descent rather than letting it pull you down.

name: Machine Row
formCues: Sit with your chest against the pad and grip the handles at a comfortable width.
Pull the handles toward your torso, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Extend your arms fully between reps, letting your shoulder blades spread.
commonMistakes: Leaning back away from the chest pad to get more range.
Shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Only pulling with the arms and not retracting the shoulder blades.

name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
formCues: Set a bench to 30–45° and lie face down on it, a dumbbell in each hand hanging straight down.
Row both dumbbells up toward your hips, leading with your elbows.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower slowly to a full arm extension and repeat.
commonMistakes: Craning the neck off the pad to look forward.
Rowing to the shoulders rather than the hips, which engages the traps too heavily.
Swinging the dumbbells using momentum since the chest is supported.

name: Chin-Up
formCues: Hang from the bar with palms facing you, hands shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Start from a dead hang with shoulders engaged.
Pull your chin above the bar, driving your elbows down and back.
Lower all the way to a dead hang with control.
commonMistakes: Half reps that barely move the shoulders.
Kipping or swinging to get the chin over the bar.
Letting the elbows flare wide, shifting work away from the lats.

name: Assisted Pull-Up
formCues: Set the counterweight high enough that you can complete full reps with good form.
Grip the handles just outside shoulder-width and kneel or stand on the platform.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the handles, driving elbows down.
Lower under control to full arm extension before the next rep.
commonMistakes: Too much counterweight, making it so easy that no real strength is built.
Short reps — always go to a full dead hang at the bottom.
Letting the platform shoot up fast on the way down.

name: Pec Deck
formCues: Sit with your back flat on the pad, forearms resting on the pads at chest height.
Squeeze your arms together in a smooth arc, as if hugging a large tree.
Pause briefly when the pads nearly touch in front of your chest.
Open back slowly, stopping before you feel shoulder tension.
commonMistakes: Seat too low, turning the arc into a shoulder-dominant movement.
Slamming the pads together at the end of each rep.
Letting the pads pull the arms back too far behind the torso, straining the shoulders.

name: Machine Lateral Raise
formCues: Sit and position the pads against your forearms just above the wrist.
Raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height in a smooth arc.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control.
Keep a slight soft bend in the elbows throughout.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the traps up to help the raise.
Going far above shoulder height, which adds no delt benefit and loads the joints.
Letting the weight slam down instead of controlling the lowering phase.

name: Hammer Curl
formCues: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Keep your elbows pinned to your ribs and curl the weights up.
Squeeze at the top without twisting the wrists.
Lower fully to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso to help get the weights up.
Rotating the wrists into a supinated curl — that defeats the neutral-grip emphasis.
Letting the elbows drift forward on the way up.

name: Preacher Curl
formCues: Sit at the preacher bench with your upper arms resting flat on the angled pad.
Grip the bar at shoulder-width and lower to a near-full extension without hyperextending.
Curl the bar up to chin height, squeezing hard at the top.
Lower slowly — the negative is especially valuable here.
commonMistakes: Lifting the upper arms off the pad, which removes the isolation.
Dropping the weight too fast on the way down.
Hyperextending the elbows at the bottom, which risks ligament strain.

name: Skull Crusher
formCues: Lie back on a flat bench, pressing the bar up so your arms are vertical over your shoulders.
Keeping your upper arms still, lower the bar by bending only at the elbows toward your forehead.
Stop just before the bar touches your forehead and extend back to straight arms.
Upper arms must stay vertical throughout — don't let them drift back.
commonMistakes: Upper arms drifting back, reducing triceps isolation and loading the shoulder.
Elbows flaring out wide instead of staying parallel.
Using too much weight and shortening the range of motion.

name: Cable Crunch
formCues: Attach a rope to a high cable, kneel facing the stack, and hold the rope at the sides of your head.
Curl your torso down toward your thighs by flexing your spine — not by hip flexion.
Squeeze your abs hard at the bottom, rounding the lower back.
Return slowly, feeling your abs lengthen under tension.
commonMistakes: Pulling the weight down with the hip flexors by bowing forward from the hips.
Holding the rope at the neck and pulling with the arms.
Moving too fast, using momentum rather than muscle contraction.

name: Seated Calf Raise
formCues: Sit with the knee pad just above your knees, balls of your feet on the platform, heels hanging.
Lower your heels as far as you can for a full stretch.
Press up onto your toes as high as possible and pause.
Lower slowly — the stretch at the bottom is where the growth happens.
commonMistakes: Bouncing out of the bottom instead of pausing in the stretch.
Short range of motion that never fully raises or lowers.
Knee pad set too tight, restricting the movement.

name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
formCues: Stand on one foot with the ball of that foot on an edge, heel hanging off.
Hold a wall or rail lightly for balance only — not to push yourself up.
Lower your heel to a deep stretch, then press up as high as you can.
Pause at the top, lower slowly, and complete all reps before switching feet.
commonMistakes: Using the support to reduce the load rather than just for balance.
Tiny bouncy reps without a full stretch or full rise.
Bending the knee, which shifts work to the soleus and reduces full calf range.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
formCues: Lie perpendicular across a bench with only your upper back on it, hips bridged up.
Hold one dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms nearly straight.
Lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head until you feel a deep lat stretch.
Pull it back up through the same arc to the starting position.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows too much, which makes it more of a triceps exercise.
Letting the hips drop instead of staying bridged.
Going too heavy and straining the shoulder joint at the bottom.

name: Side Plank
formCues: Lie on your side, forearm on the floor and elbow under your shoulder.
Lift your hips so your body forms a straight line from head to feet.
Keep your top hip stacked directly over your bottom hip.
Breathe steadily and hold — lower when your hips start to sag.
commonMistakes: Top hip rolling forward or backward, reducing lateral core demand.
Hips sagging down toward the floor.
Looking down at the floor instead of maintaining a neutral neck.

name: Bird Dog
formCues: Start on hands and knees with a neutral spine, hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace your core, then extend one arm and the opposite leg simultaneously.
Hold for two seconds at full extension without rotating the hips or pelvis.
Return with control and switch sides.
commonMistakes: Rotating the hips to swing the leg higher than parallel.
Rushing the movement rather than moving from a stable base.
Looking up and extending the neck instead of keeping a neutral spine.

name: Hollow Hold
formCues: Lie on your back and press your lower back firmly into the floor.
Lift your shoulders and legs off the floor while reaching your arms overhead.
Keep your lower back flat — not arched — throughout the hold.
Breathe steadily; if your back lifts, tuck the knees to reduce the lever arm.
commonMistakes: Lower back arching off the floor, which shifts stress to the spine.
Holding the breath instead of breathing through the hold.
Raising the legs too high, which reduces the challenge on the anterior core.

name: Farmer Carry
formCues: Pick up a heavy kettlebell in each hand using a safe hinge.
Stand tall with shoulders back and down, core braced.
Walk with deliberate steps, keeping your torso from tilting side to side.
Set the weights down by hinging under control at the end of each length.
commonMistakes: Torso leaning to one side when the load is uneven or too heavy.
Shrugging the shoulders up instead of keeping them packed.
Fast, shuffling steps that prevent a stable, upright trunk position.

name: Dumbbell Farmer Carry
formCues: Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a safe deadlift hinge.
Stand fully upright, core braced and shoulders retracted.
Walk at a steady pace with deliberate foot placement.
Lower the dumbbells with control at the end of each set length.
commonMistakes: Trunk tilting toward the heavier side.
Shrugging continuously rather than keeping the shoulder blades set.
Short choppy steps that encourage a side-to-side sway.
